name: Table
summary: Data table with sortable columns, smart height/auto-pagination, and unified `selectionMode` (`'none'|'single'|'multiple'`) keyed by `getItemKey` for selection that survives data refreshes; supports controlled or uncontrolled selection (`selected`/`defaultSelected`/`onSelectionChange`, row arrays). Use it for tabular data with columns.

bestPractices: Keep tables readable: enable `striped` and/or `hoverable` for large datasets, but avoid heavy styling that competes with content.

Prefer smart height over tiny scrollbars: enable `constrainHeight` and set `minConstrainedRows` (default 4). If the viewport would show fewer rows, the table expands; when the dataset exceeds `maxExpandedRows` (default 30), it paginates instead of showing a tiny scroller.
Provide an initial sort (`initialSort`), align numeric columns to the right, and format dates consistently.

Enable selection only when follow‑up actions operate on selected rows; use `selectionMode` (`'none' | 'single' | 'multiple'`) to match the action design. Selection can be controlled (`selected` + `onSelectionChange`) or uncontrolled (`defaultSelected`); both are row arrays of `T`. In `'multiple'` mode the header shows a select-all checkbox (with an indeterminate partial state).

When the table is selectable and the data is refreshed immutably (new row objects fetched or re‑mapped), set `getItemKey` (a property name like `getItemKey="id"` or a function `getItemKey={(row) => row.id}`) so selection is tracked by a stable key and survives the refresh. Without it, selection falls back to object identity and is cleared whenever the row objects are replaced.

When `onRowClick` is set, rows become keyboard-operable (focusable, Enter/Space activate) with a focus ring and a ≥44px coarse-pointer height; clicks originating from interactive cell content (links/buttons/inputs) are ignored so nested controls keep working.
Keep column count reasonable on narrow screens; prefer fewer, more meaningful columns over many cramped ones. Use succinct headers.
Expose `onRowClick` when rows drive navigation or drill‑ins so the parent surface can react without wiring bespoke click handlers into each cell.

Single‑select with initial sort
Selection that survives a data refresh (getItemKey)

Row click actions
